questions.question: Define the Graph ADT. Explain the different types of graphs with examples -- directed, undirected, weighted, and complete graphs.
keyPoints: Formal definition G = (V, E)
Undirected graph: edges are unordered pairs
Directed graph (digraph): edges are ordered pairs
Weighted graph: edges carry numerical cost/weight
Complete graph Kn: every vertex pair connected, n(n-1)/2 edges
Examples and diagrams for each type

questions.question: Explain the following graph terminologies with suitable examples: degree, in-degree, out-degree, path, cycle, and connected graph.
keyPoints: Degree of a vertex: number of edges incident on it
In-degree and out-degree for digraphs
Handshaking theorem: sum of degrees = 2|E|
Path: sequence of vertices connected by edges
Simple path vs non-simple path
Cycle: path that starts and ends at same vertex

questions.question: State and prove the Handshaking Theorem for undirected graphs.
keyPoints: Statement: sum of degrees = 2 * number of edges
Each edge (u,v) contributes 1 to deg(u) and 1 to deg(v)
Therefore each edge contributes exactly 2 to total degree sum
Corollary: number of odd-degree vertices is even

questions.question: Differentiate between a graph and a tree. Show that a tree with n vertices has exactly n-1 edges.
keyPoints: A tree is a connected acyclic graph
A graph can have cycles and be disconnected
Proof by induction that tree with n vertices has n-1 edges
Base case: single vertex, 0 edges
Removing a leaf gives tree with n-1 vertices and n-2 edges

questions.question: For the given graph with vertices {A, B, C, D, E} and edges {(A,B), (A,C), (B,C), (B,D), (C,D), (D,E)}, find the degree of each vertex and verify the Handshaking Theorem.
keyPoints: Compute degree of each vertex from adjacency
deg(A)=2, deg(B)=3, deg(C)=3, deg(D)=3, deg(E)=1
Sum of degrees = 2+3+3+3+1 = 12
Number of edges = 6, so 2*6 = 12 -- verified
